mnemonics.title: Randomized Select Steps
mnemonics.content: PARK: Partition, Adjust k if needed, Recurse into one side, Keep going until found

acronyms.usage: Algorithm for finding k-th smallest element in O(n) average time

acronyms.expansion: Pivot Around element divide into LESS and GREATER than pivot
acronyms.usage: The key operation in both QuickSort and Randomized Select

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a cafeteria line where people (elements) stand on either side of a chosen person (pivot) - shorter people to the left, taller to the right. The pivot finds their correct position in the line.
Think of a tree where you only go down ONE branch - the branch containing your target (k-th element). You don't explore the other branch at all, unlike QuickSort which explores both.
If finding 5th smallest in 10 items, imagine cutting a deck of cards - after partition, you only look at the pile where the 5th card could be, discarding the rest.

analogies.analogy: Like finding the median-height student in a class without lining everyone up by height. You randomly pick a student as reference, have others stand on left or right, then only check the side where the median should be.
Similar to searching for a word in a dictionary by randomly opening a page instead of always opening from the beginning - you avoid the worst case of the word being at the very end.
Like playing 20 questions but only asking one direction of questions - once you know the answer is in one half, you completely ignore the other half.
